# forgejo_repository_webhook (Resource)

Forgejo repository webhook resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    forgejo = {
      source = "svalabs/forgejo"
    }
  }
}

provider "forgejo" {
  host = "http://localhost:3000"
}

resource "forgejo_repository" "example" {
  name = "example_repo"
}

resource "forgejo_repository_webhook" "kennel" {
  repository_id = forgejo_repository.example.id
  type          = "forgejo"
  url           = "https://kennel.example.org/webhook"
  secret        = "my-webhook-secret"
  content_type  = "json"
  active        = true
  events        = ["push", "pull_request"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `events` (List of String) List of events that trigger the webhook.
- `repository_id` (Number) Numeric identifier of the repository. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.
- `type` (String) Type of the webhook (forgejo, slack, discord, etc.). Changing this forces a new resource to be created.
- `url` (String) Target URL for the webhook.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) Whether the webhook is active. Defaults to `true`.
- `content_type` (String) Content type for the webhook payload (json or form).
- `secret` (String, Sensitive) Secret used to sign webhook payloads.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Time at which the webhook was created.
- `hook_id` (Number) Numeric identifier of the webhook.
